name: Operation Shadow Grid — Detection Coverage
description: Detection coverage for Operation Shadow Grid — 14 techniques across 8 tactics. Red = detected with real attack data. Orange = staged rule (technique not exercised in this engagement). Yellow = tested, not vulnerable.

techniques.comment: Detection 1 — Nmap SYN scan detected via Zeek conn.log (129,038 REJ connections). Gobuster detected via Zeek http.log (404 floods).
Detection 2 — SQL injection payload (UNION SELECT) captured in Zeek http.log URI. Command injection POST requests captured in Apache syslog.
Detection 2/15 — Command injection and reverse shell. POST requests to /vulnerabilities/exec/ in Apache syslog. Reverse shell closed with host-based syslog Detection 15.
Detection 4 — Cron backdoor (www-data reverse shell every 5 min) detected in Linux syslog cron execution events.
Detection 4 — Web shell (info.php?cmd=whoami) detected in both Zeek http.log and Apache syslog. curl/8.17.0 user-agent.
Detection 6 — Password reuse (www-data → sysadmin via su) detected in Linux auth syslog. Session opened uid=33 → uid=1000.
Detection 6 — Sudo escalation (sysadmin → root via sudo su -) detected in Linux auth syslog. COMMAND=/usr/bin/su -, uid=1000 → uid=0.
Detection 8 — SMBExec lateral movement. Event 4624 Type 3 (NTLM, Administrator, Elevated_Token=Yes) + Event 4672 (SYSTEM privileges) + Zeek conn.log SMB to port 445.
Detection 9 — Kerberoasting. Event 4769 with Ticket_Encryption_Type=0x17 (RC4) for Service_Name=svc_web, requested by j.smith.
Detection 10 — DCSync. Event 4662 with DS-Replication-Get-Changes-All GUID {1131f6ad-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2}, Account_Name=Administrator. Full domain hash dump.
Detection 13 — DNS tunneling exfiltration. 116 Zeek dns.log queries with long base64 subdomains to evil-corp.com, all SERVFAIL.
AS-REP Roasting — tested with impacket-GetNPUsers. No accounts vulnerable (all had Kerberos pre-authentication enabled). Documented as negative coverage.
Staged rule — PowerShell Abuse detection rule written and tuned. Technique not exercised in this engagement (attack chain used Unix shell, not PowerShell).
Staged rule — Brute Force detection rule written and tuned. Technique not exercised in this engagement (used password reuse, not brute force).
Staged rule — RDP Lateral Movement detection rule written and tuned. Technique not exercised (used SMBExec instead of RDP).
